old_claim.note: Electronics provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: Implantable pacemakers require electronic pulse-generator circuitry to deliver controlled pacing impulses.

source_shape.source_locator: VA history describing the Chardack-Greatbatch implantable pacemaker pulse generator and electrode work.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source describes an implanted electrode connected to a pulse generator refined into an implantable unit for long-term correction of heart block.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: This supports a required electronics edge because the pacemaker's therapeutic function is controlled electrical pulse generation delivered to the heart.

ontology_before: Pacemaker electronics were modeled as a generic enabler.
ontology_after: Electronics is now the hard pulse-generator foundation for implantable pacemakers.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: pacemaker electronics is required rather than generic enabling.
would_reject_if: The node were re-scoped to biological pacemakers rather than electronic implantable pacemakers.
A source showed implantable electronic pacemakers do not require pulse-generator circuitry.
The graph removed battery power while strengthening the electronics edge.
short_reason: An implantable pacemaker is an electronic pulse generator, so electronics is a hard component dependency.
